sections.question: Whether the law firm must report the value of legal or campaign services performed without cost for the benefit of a candidate or committee on Form 550.
sections.conclusion: Donations of legal services which would constitute in-kind contributions or independent expenditures must be reported by the law firm on its Form 550. However, volunteer personal services need not be reported, and general overhead expenses or employee salaries do not need to be reported if the employee spends less than 10% of compensated time on political activities. Any additional expenses incurred must be reported as contributions, and if such contributions equal $500 or more in a calendar year, the law firm becomes a committee subject to campaign statement filing requirements.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a law firm report in-kind donations of legal or campaign services provided at no cost to candidates or committees on its Form 550 filing?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The law firm must report in-kind donations of legal services on Form 550 as contributions, but volunteer personal services are excluded from reporting requirements. General overhead and employee salaries are also excluded unless the employee dedicates more than 10% of time to political activities.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a formal FPPC advice letter confirming telephone advice. Clear question and conclusion sections present. Minor OCR artifacts in source document (e.g., 'Caltfonya' for 'California', 'Mlelsen' for 'Nielsen') do not affect content extraction. Document ID appears to be 77-06-4/3 (internal reference) with letter designation 77ADV-77-013.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the law firm must report the value of legal or campaign services performed without cost for the benefit of a candidate or committee on Form 550.

CONCLUSION: Donations of legal services which would constitute in-kind contributions or independent expenditures must be reported by the law firm on its Form 550. However, volunteer personal services need not be reported, and general overhead expenses or employee salaries do not need to be reported if the employee spends less than 10% of compensated time on political activities. Any additional expenses incurred must be reported as contributions, and if such contributions equal $500 or more in a calendar year, the law firm becomes a committee subject to campaign statement filing requirements.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises a law firm on its reporting obligations regarding in-kind contributions of legal and campaign services provided to candidates or committees, clarifying what must be reported on Form 550 and what exemptions apply.
